questionHtml: The temperatures are now reversed so that X is at 45 °C and Z is at 90 °C. Show that the rate of energy transfer is unchanged.
markschemeHtml:
repeats calculation to show that θ = 75 °C ✓

temperature difference across YZ is still 15 K which gives the same rate of energy transfer (but in opposite direction) ✓

meta.title: The temperatures are now reversed so that X is at 45 °C and Z is at 90 °C. Show that the rate of energy transfer is unchanged.
